goal: Build a physics drop-test scene under /World/Drop: static ground plate + 3 rigid-body cubes stacked at z=1,2,3m, each with RigidBodyAPI+CollisionAPI+1kg mass. Ground has only CollisionAPI.
thoughts: Compose the scene in one pass: parent Xform → ground (size=1, scale=[5,5,0.1], only CollisionAPI) → 3 cubes (size=0.2, positioned on z, both APIs + mass). Use bulk_apply_schema to batch identical schemas across the 3 cubes. Static collider is NOT a rigid body — that distinction matters for PhysX.
code:
# Scene parent
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Drop", prim_type="Xform")

# Static ground
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Drop/Ground",
    prim_type="Cube",
    size=1.0,
    position=[0, 0, 0],
    scale=[5, 5, 0.1],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Drop/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Three dynamic cubes at z=1,2,3
for i, z in enumerate([1.0, 2.0, 3.0]):
    create_prim(
        prim_path=f"/World/Drop/Cube_{i}",
        prim_type="Cube",
        size=0.2,
        position=[0, 0, z],
    )

bulk_apply_schema(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Drop/Cube_{i}" for i in range(3)],
    api_schema="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI",
)
bulk_apply_schema(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Drop/Cube_{i}" for i in range(3)],
    api_schema="PhysicsCollisionAPI",
)
bulk_apply_schema(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Drop/Cube_{i}" for i in range(3)],
    api_schema="PhysicsMassAPI",
)
for i in range(3):
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/Drop/Cube_{i}", attr_name="physics:mass", value=1.0)
failure_modes: Giving the ground a RigidBodyAPI — it becomes dynamic and falls.
Using scale=[0.2,0.2,0.2] without passing size=0.2 or size=1.0 — USD Cube default size=2 → effective 0.4 m, not 0.2.
Forgetting PhysicsMassAPI — gravity still applies but the cube uses default computed mass, not the requested 1 kg.
Calling apply_api_schema 9 times in a loop instead of bulk_apply_schema 3 times — correct but 3x the RPC cost.
